content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN distinct points (P1,x,P1,y),(P2,x,P2,y),…,(PN,x,PN,y)(P_{1, x}, P_{1, y}), (P_{2, x}, P_{2, y}), \dots, (P_{N, x}, P_{N, y})(P1,x​,P1,y​),(P2,x​,P2,y​),…,(PN,x​,PN,y​) on the coordinate plane, and QQQ queries in the form `L R X`.

In each query, consider the polygon formed by the points in the interval [L,R][L,R][L,R] (i.e. the points (PL,x,PL,y),(PL+1,x,PL+1,y),…,(PR,x,PR,y)(P_{L, x}, P_{L, y}), (P_{L+1, x}, P_{L+1, y}), \dots, (P_{R, x}, P_{R, y})(PL,x​,PL,y​),(PL+1,x​,PL+1,y​),…,(PR,x​,PR,y​)), along with (0,0)(0,0)(0,0) and (X,0)(X,0)(X,0).


If this polygon is not convex, that is the polygon contains an angle **greater** than 180∘180^{\circ}180∘, output −1-1−1.
Otherwise, the polygon can be dissected into R−L+1R-L+1R−L+1 triangles that all contain (X,0)(X,0)(X,0) as a vertex. Out of these triangles, consider the triangle with the maximum area, and let this area be AAA. Output 2A2A2A.

Input Format

The first line contains one integer NNN - the number of points.
The iii-th line of the next NNN lines contain two integers Pi,xP_{i, x}Pi,x​ and Pi,yP_{i, y}Pi,y​ - the xxx and yyy coordinate of the iii-th point.
The next line contains one integer QQQ - the number of queries.
Each of the next QQQ lines contain three integers LLL, RRR, and XXX representing a query.


Output Format
For each query, output on a single line −1-1−1 if the polygon is not convex, otherwise output 2A2A2A, where AAA is the area of the largest triangle the polygon can be dissected into per the statement's description.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer N – the number of distinct points.
The next N lines each contain two integers Pi_x and Pi_y – the coordinates of the i‑th point.
The following line contains an integer Q – the number of queries.
Each of the next Q lines contains three integers L, R and X describing a query.
All points are given in the order they must be used when building the polygon.
content.outputFormat:
For each query output a single line.
If the polygon formed by (0,0), PL … PR and (X,0) is not convex output -1.
Otherwise output the value 2·A where A is the maximal area of a triangle that can be obtained by dissecting the polygon into triangles that all share the vertex (X,0). The answer is always an integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111:


In the diagram above, note how the polygon can be dissected into 222 triangles that both include vertex (X,0)(X,0)(X,0). The purple triangle has an area of 333 and the blue triangle has an area of 444.
Therefore, 2A=2×4=82A = 2 \times 4 = 82A=2×4=8.

solution.approach:
For a query we have the polygon (0,0) – PL – PL+1 – … – PR – (X,0). The polygon is convex iff:
1. The chain PL … PR itself is convex. This can be checked by ensuring that for every interior triple (i‑1,i,i+1) the cross product has the same sign. We pre‑compute an array bad[i] (1 if the triple (i‑1,i,i+1) makes a non‑convex turn) and a prefix sum prefBad. Then the chain is convex exactly when prefBad[R‑1]‑prefBad[L] == 0.
2. The turn at the left end (0,0), PL, PL+1 is convex. Its cross product is crossL = PL.x·PL+1.y – PL.y·PL+1.x and must be ≥ 0.
3. The turn at the right end PR‑1, PR, (X,0) is convex. Its cross product is crossR = (PR‑1.x‑X)·PR.y – (PR‑1.y)·(PR.x‑X) and must be ≥ 0.
If any of the three conditions fails we output -1.
When the polygon is convex we have to find the largest triangle among the fan triangles (X,0)–Pi–Pi+1 (L ≤ i < R). The doubled area of such a triangle is | (Pi.x‑X)·Pi+1.y – (Pi+1.x‑X)·Pi.y |. Expanding gives a linear function in X: f_i(X) = a_i + b_i·X where a_i = Pi.x·Pi+1.y – Pi+1.x·Pi.y and b_i = Pi.y – Pi+1.y.
Thus the answer for a query is max_{i∈[L,R‑1]} |a_i + b_i·X| = max( max_i (a_i + b_i·X),  - min_i (a_i + b_i·X) ).
We need to obtain the maximum and minimum of a set of lines at a given X over an arbitrary interval. This is a classic offline problem solved with a segment tree where each node stores a Li Chao tree (dynamic convex hull trick) of the lines belonging to its segment. Building the structure costs O(N log N). A query over interval [L,R‑1] visits O(log N) nodes; each node answers a maximum in O(log C) where C is the coordinate range of X (≤1e9). The overall query time is O(log^2 N), fast enough for Q = 2·10^5.
Special case L == R: the polygon is a triangle (0,0) – PL – (X,0) which is always convex. Its doubled area equals PL.y·X.
The algorithm therefore works in:
• Pre‑processing: O(N log N) time, O(N log N) memory for the segment tree of Li Chao trees.
• Each query: O(log^2 N) time, O(1) extra memory.
All arithmetic fits in 64‑bit signed integers because |a_i| ≤ 10^10 and |b_i·X| ≤ 10^5·10^9 = 10^14.
solution.timeComplexity: Pre‑processing O(N log N). Each query O(log^2 N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N log N) for the segment tree of Li Chao trees (each line appears in O(log N) nodes).
